title: Check for K5 Subdivision
description: Algorithm to check if a graph contains K5 as a subdivision (topological minor)
code:
ALGORITHM ContainsK5Subdivision(G)
BEGIN
 // K5 subdivision means 5 vertices each 
 // connected to each other (directly or via paths)
 // with no shared internal vertices
 
 FOR each set of 5 vertices S in G DO
 IF CheckK5Structure(G, S) THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN FALSE
END

FUNCTION CheckK5Structure(G, S)
BEGIN
 // For each pair in S, check if there exists 
 // a path connecting them without using 
 // other vertices from S as internal nodes
 FOR each pair (u, v) in S DO
 IF NOT PathExistsWithoutUsing(G, u, v, S) THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN TRUE
END
explanation: This algorithm checks whether a graph contains a subdivision of K5 by examining all sets of 5 vertices and verifying if each pair can be connected via paths that don't use other selected vertices as internal nodes.

title: Kuratowski Planarity Test
description: Algorithm applying Kuratowski's Theorem to test planarity
code:
ALGORITHM IsPlanarKuratowski(G)
// Uses Kuratowski's Theorem
// Graph is planar iff no K5 or K3,3 subdivision
BEGIN
 n ← |V(G)|
 
 // Quick bounds check (necessary but not sufficient)
 IF n ≥ 5 THEN
 // Check for K5 subdivision
 IF ContainsK5Subdivision(G) THEN
 RETURN FALSE // Non-planar
 END IF
 END IF
 
 IF n ≥ 6 THEN
 // Check for K3,3 subdivision
 IF ContainsK33Subdivision(G) THEN
 RETURN FALSE // Non-planar
 END IF
 END IF
 
 RETURN TRUE // Possibly planar
END

FUNCTION ContainsK33Subdivision(G)
BEGIN
 // Check for two sets of 3 vertices with 
 // all 9 paths connecting them without 
 // sharing internal vertices
 FOR each bipartition (A, B) where |A|=|B|=3 DO
 IF CheckK33Structure(G, A, B) THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN FALSE
END
explanation: This algorithm implements Kuratowski's Theorem by checking for the presence of forbidden subdivisions (K5 or K3,3). If either is found, the graph is non-planar.

title: Edge Subdivision Operation
description: Algorithm to perform edge subdivision on a graph
code:
ALGORITHM SubdivideEdge(G, u, v)
// Replaces edge (u,v) with path u-w-v
// where w is a new vertex
BEGIN
 w ← NewVertex()
 AddVertex(G, w)
 RemoveEdge(G, u, v)
 AddEdge(G, u, w)
 AddEdge(G, w, v)
 RETURN G
END

ALGORITHM CreateSubdivisionGraph(G, edgeList)
// Creates a subdivision of G by subdividing
// edges in edgeList
BEGIN
 G' ← Copy(G)
 FOR each (u, v) in edgeList DO
 G' ← SubdivideEdge(G', u, v)
 END FOR
 RETURN G'
END
explanation: These algorithms demonstrate the edge subdivision operation, which is fundamental to the concept of homeomorphic graphs in Kuratowski's Theorem.

complexity.time: O(k) where k is number of subdivided edges
